content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 5, 1987

Linda L. Anderson
Iowa Campaign Finance Disclosure Commission
507-10th Street, 7th Floor
Des Moines, IA 50309

Re: Our File No. I-87-329

Dear Ms. Anderson:

We have received your letter requesting copies of record
systems and other information concerning public access to
campaign disclosure statements in the State of California.

Pursuant to the Political Reform Act (Cal. Government Code
Sections 81000-91015), all campaign disclosure statements are
public records. In this regard, California Government Code
Section 81008 provides as follows:

81008. Public Records; Inspection; Reproduction.
Every report and statement filed pursuant to this
title is a public record open for public inspection
and reproduction during regular business hours,
commencing as soon as practicable, but in any event
not later than the second business day following the
day on which it was received. No conditions
whatsoever shall be imposed upon persons desiring to
inspect or reproduce reports and statements filed
under this title, nor shall any information or
identification be required from such persons. Copies
shall be provided at a charge not to exceed ten cents
($0.10) per page. Campaign statements shall be open
for public inspection and reproduction from 9:00 a.m.
to 5:00 p.m. on the Saturday preceding an election
held on the first Tuesday after the first Monday in
June or November in even-numbered years in the offices
of Secretary of State, Registrar-Recorder of Los
Angeles County, Registrar of Voters of San Diego
County and Registrar of Voters of the City and County
of San Francisco.

Accordingly, the Commission's information manual on
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act
contains the enclosed statement concerning information
collection and access.

428 I Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Linda L. Anderson
January 5, 1987
Page 2


     Recently, we have had to face questions about public
access to the Commission's investigative files under
California's Public Records Act. (Cal. Gov. Code Section
6250, et seq.) Enclosed is a copy of a court decision and
a new Commission regulation on this subject.

     Please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if I can be of any
further assistance.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [Signature]
                                        By: Kathryn E. Donovan
                                            Counsel, Legal Division


KED:da

STATE OF

IOWA
TERRY E. BRANSTAD, GOVERNOR                    CAMPAIGN FINANCE DISCLOSURE COMMISSION
                                                                    KAY WILLIAMS, EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR


                                          December 8, 1987




Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California  95814

To Whom It May Concern:

     It is my understanding that your state has an act which relates to fair
information practices. Iowa agencies are now in the process of adopting rules
which will implement the Iowa Fair Information Practices Act and as this is
new to us, I am looking to other states for any guidance you may be able to
provide. The area which causes us the most difficulty is as follows:

     "A description of which of its records are public records, which are
confidential records and which are partially public and partially confidential
records . . . The description shall indicate whether the records contain
personally identifiable information."

     If your state has defined and described record systems which relate to
campaign finance disclosure, would you please provide us with copies? Any
other information which you feel might be helpful would also be appreciated.

     We are working with a deadline, so I would be grateful for a prompt response.
Thank you in advance for your assistance.

     Sincerely,

     [signature]
     Linda L. Andersen
     Secretary to the Director

lla




507 · 10TH STREET, 7TH FLOOR / DES MOINES, IOWA 50309 / TELEPHONE 515-281-4411

sections.question_synthetic: The Iowa Campaign Finance Disclosure Commission requested information about California's public access policies for campaign disclosure statements and record systems definitions under fair information practices laws.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provided information on California Government Code Section 81008, which establishes that all campaign disclosure statements are public records open for inspection and reproduction with no conditions imposed, available at ten cents per page during regular business hours and specifically on Saturdays before certain elections.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is correspondence consisting of two letters: an incoming request dated December 8, 1987 followed by the FPPC response dated January 5, 1987 (appears to be misfiled chronologically). No traditional QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present; structured as informational correspondence. File number I-87-329.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The Iowa Campaign Finance Disclosure Commission requested information about California's public access policies for campaign disclosure statements and record systems definitions under fair information practices laws.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provided information on California Government Code Section 81008, which establishes that all campaign disclosure statements are public records open for inspection and reproduction with no conditions imposed, available at ten cents per page during regular business hours and specifically on Saturdays before certain elections.

embedding.summary: This document contains an incoming request from the Iowa Campaign Finance Disclosure Commission to the California FPPC seeking information about public access to campaign disclosure records and record system definitions, followed by the FPPC's response providing relevant statutory provisions and enclosing additional materials.
